rationale: Both candidates land the same core diagnosis and disposition as the thread (regression from #4527's `(None, per_req_payloads)` split fixed by #4792, already in the 0.24/a560ed18 rebase, workaround via async_chunk:true) — Y's fix snippet for gpu_generation_model_runner.py even matches the ground-truth comment verbatim. X goes further: it builds the full causal chain (stage registered as full-payload consumer → WAITING_FOR_INPUT → accumulate_full_payload_output skipped → connector timeout → FINISHED_ERROR) with many file:line citations, explicitly ties the explanation to the reporter's specific log/env details (timeout message, red-herring NVFP4 patch and #4962 note), and plausibly differentiates variable naming between the AR and generation runners rather than assuming identical code. Y is correct but thinner on mechanism, reuses identical variable names across both runner files without justification (less credible), and leaks raw internal task-metadata/report scaffolding into what should be a clean maintainer reply, hurting completeness/polish.
